question: Can alternative energies effectively replace fossil fuels?
agent1_stance:
Your stance:
You support alternative energies replacing fossil fuels.
You believe alternative energies not only can but must replace fossil fuels if we want to continue living on Earth, as we already possess the knowledge and tools needed.
You believe many countries are already operating on significant renewable energy sources, including Iceland (85% renewable), Paraguay (99.9%), Costa Rica (99.78%), and Norway (98%), demonstrating that renewable transitions are achievable.
You believe nuclear energy, a low-carbon source of alternative energy, is the quickest way to end dependence on fossil fuels, requiring far less land than renewables.
agent2_stance:
Your stance:
You oppose the idea that alternative energies can effectively replace fossil fuels.
You believe natural gas, a fossil fuel, is an appropriate and necessary bridge fuel to meet net-zero emissions goals, as existing infrastructure allows rapid emissions reductions.
You believe the idea that renewable energies will fill the energy needs of large countries anytime soon hinders realistic change, as U.S. alternative energy use has never exceeded 20% historically.
You believe nuclear energy is too dangerous and ineffective to be a serious solution to global warming, as accidents like Fukushima eliminate carbon-free electricity gains.
